programmes.show_description: It's the day of Minnie's mother's funeral. After the ceremony, Alf finds he has a shocking cough. S6 E5: The Wake
With The Police. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
A visiting diplomat is of interest to several people because of her pearl necklace. S3 E3: Jeannine

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
By 1915 trench warfare had stalled, Naval and amphibious plans had failed disastrously, revealing why the Dardanelles campaign collapsed. Narrated by Robert Powell.
We compare the elite teams at the frontlines of the fight against terror against past battles comparing American riflemen with British line soldiers and the story of American sniper Timothy Murphy.
As the armies of World War I struggled under a barrage of terrifying weapons, the German government turned to covert operations to break the stalemate. S2 E10: WW1 - Germany's Secret Gambles
A look at London's first 1,000 years includes treasures unearthed by Crossrail, the ruins of Roman Londinium, handwritten messages dating back 2,000 years and the first London Bridge. Ep1
The RAF had aircraft that could escort bombers to targets deep inside Germany and back by the end of World War One, and the Hawker Hunter and F-86 Sabre took the RAF supersonic. Ep3: A Vicious Sting
The Royal Navy fought a long campaign against the German U-boats and surface ships in the Atlantic; its Pacific Fleet helped win victory over Japan. Ep10: The Royal Navy

We examine the course of savage fighting in the Balkans in the latter half of the conflict in World War 2 where after the uprising by the Romanians, the Soviet army moved swiftly to capture Bucharest.
The story of the men that cracked the Nazis' Enigma code, from Polish mathematicians to the establishment at Bletchley Park and the work of Alan Turing. Ep4: The Men Who Cracked Enigma
Fast patrol boats armed with torpedoes and machine guns were the marine raiders of World War II; this programme looks at some of their most spectacular raids. Ep10: Fast Attack Boats
Devastation touches the civilians of Germany; with the US as a newly-engaged ally, Western forces bring the war to the Axis front but at extreme costs. Ep5: Bombing The Reich
Battles in WWII occur in freezing temperatures, humid jungles, snowy hills and arid deserts; Allied and Japanese forces struggle for control of Port Moresby. Ep7: Multidimensional Warfare
We take a look at one of WWII's most significant theatres of warfare, in which Germany's U-boat fleet relentlessly attacked Britain's supply lines. S1 E6: Battle of the Atlantic
Elizabeth and Lawrence's marriage appears to have ended as it becomes apparent that her visit for a simple tea has more to it than first meets the eye. S2 E4: Whom God Hath Joined
A local property developer offers to buy Arthur's lock-up and car lot, but Terry suspects that the deal is bent and turns to DS Jones for help. S7 E6: The Wrong Goodbye
Dempsey and Makepeace pose as Middle East terrorists to find an arms dealer, and Dempsey undertakes a routine psychological assessment. S3 E4: Extreme Prejudice
A visitor from Gene Hunt's old stomping ground of Manchester has a surprise in store, and Alex is desperate to get to the bottom of the corruption she believes is in the force. S2 E4
Alf is charged to look after the Christmas club subscriptions at the pub. Unfortunately maths is not his strong point and he struggles to balance the books. S6 E6: Christmas Club Books
With BA Robertson and Mel Smith. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy show from Thames TV, all in the best possible taste!
Simon is visited by a petty crook who has become involved in a blackmail plot by a villain known only as the Scorpion. S3 E4: The Scorpion

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
Amelia Earhart inspired millions, then vanished on her Pacific flight. Her fate remains one of aviation's enduring mysteries. Narrated by Robert Powell.
Our exploration of the elite teams that have led the USA's battle against terror continues with a look at the US Special Forces and the founding of the legendary Green Berets and Navy SEALs.
From Germany's Big Bertha in World War I to Iraq's top-secret Project Babylon, we look at secret cannons designed to hit their targets from miles away. S2 E11: WW1 - Super Guns
Exploring medieval London, what Westminster Abbey reveals about the competition between the crown and the city, the cellars below the Ministry of Defence and artefacts owned by Henry VIII. Ep2
The RAF had bombers that could fly to Berlin and back by the end of the First World War, but British civilians found themselves under direct attack by Zeppelin airships. Ep4: Strike Hard, Strike Sure
This programme takes a look at the illustrious history of the Chindits, a group formed by Major General Orde Wingate to fight the Japanese in Burma. Ep11: The Chindits
Tim Pigott-Smith narrates the story of the battle for Guadalcanal and the capture of this strategically crucial island in World War 2 during which more than 25,000 Japanese lost their lives.
The story of Nazi heavy cruisers and battleships during WWII including the Bismarck, how she got into the Atlantic, and the steps taken to defeat her. Ep5: The Men Who Sank The Bismarck
...Where Were You? There are some news events that change the world. In today's episode we take you back to July 25th 2000 and the fatal crash of the Air France Concorde flight 4590.
Japan begins to conquer southeast Asia as the Allies attempt to block the empire's aggressive expansion, but ruthless tactics by Japan only help to further their war effort. Ep6: Road to Burma
A handful of names are carved into history as turning points which influenced the course of WWII. These words are indestructible: El Alamein, Okinawa, and Pearl Harbor. Ep8: Turning Points of History
After Hitler invaded Poland, the Western Allies and Nazi Germany were technically at war - for 7 months, neither side engaged in major military land operations. S1 E7: 'Sitzkrieg' and the Phoney War
King Edward VII is the guest of honour at Eaton Place and Mrs Bridges gets to do what every cook in Mayfair and Belgravia would wish to do: cook for His Royal Highness. S2 E5: Guest of Honour
After Terry goes missing, Arthur Daley returns with his nephew Ray in tow as his new minder - and ambitions to expand into the European market. S8 E1: The Loneliness of the Long Distance Entrepreneur
When drug dealers kidnap a woman's child to deter her from testifying against them, Dempsey must turn to the villainous Bogeyman for help. S3 E5: Bird of Prey
When a violent burglary occurs at Alex's in-laws' house, she comes face-to-face with their son Peter, the 14-year-old future father of Molly. S2 E5
